response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is a significant mismatch for this role. Professional C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) on a C IDE tooling project, far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. There is no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain experience in a C++ context — the RIDE Capital role involved investment-related fintech but exclusively used JavaScript/TypeScript. SQL Server and Oracle experience are not evidenced, and the overall profile is oriented toward web services, developer tooling, and open-source GIS work.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps practices (GitLab CI, Docker, trunk-based development) align with desirable pipeline knowledge
Exposure to high-performance, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic shows awareness of performance-critical engineering
Brief professional C++ work at National Instruments indicates foundational language familiarity
concerns: C++ professional experience totals roughly 6 months versus the 5–10 years explicitly required; remaining tenure is JavaScript, TypeScript, D, and Node.js
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge is present anywhere in the resume
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; SQL is listed generically in skills with no supporting project detail

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / fixed income domain absence
